src: At about the same time the aliens department of the Home Office took on extra staff and moved to Cleveland House on Thorney Street.

src: I give my leg a nasty bash, but I don't stop and see if it's bleeding — I just want to get out.

src: Mrs Aggie, I don't want to go to a school where I won't be able to get out and come home.

src: Whoever takes on the trout farm will have their work cut out.

src: You want to get out as soon as you get in that's nice

src: It's so easy for problems to get out of proportion — most conflicts can be overcome by thoughtful action.

src: The only thing to do was to hold her emotions in check and get on with her work.

src: Such MPs would, it is true, know that territory better than any incoming outsider, and being known there would presumably get off to a flying start in the pursuit of popularity.

src: He was under immense pressure from the US to give in and back no holds barred military action by UN forces in the former Yugoslav republic.

src: She never got on with Dad, he was too sublime.

src: You see, it appears that they got on well enough.

src: On Hong Kong this year it took on a slightly different connotation.

src: As it is difficult for people to get together, he is hoping to establish a telephone conference system.

src: We get on with life and take pleasure in things whether she's ill or not.

src: At the initial sessions, a registry sheet is made out and photos and videos are made.

src: It was jealousy, I suppose; the fact that I had spotted a chance they had not, although I couldn't get over the suspicion that their reaction to me had something to do with how Liza had told the story.

src: I was told that only about one-quarter of the students at institutes of higher education in Beijing were actually getting on with their studies and many of these were motivated solely by the desire to go abroad.
mt: "Me dijeron que solo alrededor de un cuarto de los estudiantes en las instituciones de educación superior en Beijing realmente estaban avanzando con sus estudios, y muchos de ellos estaban motivados únicamente por el deseo de irse al extranjero."

src: And I put balanced it out across this, and I set my camera down er down below, on a time exposure which runs off in about one minute, so I had one minute to run up the side of the thing and get out here and balance myself.
mt: "Y colo la cosa equilibrada a lo largo de esto, y colo mi cámara abajo, debajo, en una exposición de tiempo que dura aproximadamente un minuto, así que tuve un minuto para subir por el lado de la cosa y equilibrarme aquí."

src: I had no desire to go out, but it would be one way of getting through the evening.
mt: "No tenía ganas de salir, pero era una forma de pasar la noche."

src: In the case of Russia, revisionist research has underlined the manner in which the specific nature of the tsarist regime conditioned the decision to take on the Central Powers.

src: Getting on with what you should be doing that can only see a few people doing that who are actually getting on with what they should have been doing while you are waiting.
mt: "Obtener lo que deberías estar haciendo, lo que solo unos pocos realmente están haciendo, mientras esperas mientras ellos están haciendo lo que deberían haber estado haciendo."

src: How many Englishmen gave in to their emotions like that?
mt: "¿Cuántos ingleses cayeron a sus emociones como ese?"

src: Court permission has been granted to sell the property and advice is being sought as to the best way to approach this but the state of the building and the effects of the economic situation do not make for easy decisions.

src: She ducked under his arm and made for the door but he caught her arm.

src: So they are observations and it is my feeling that this motion has taken on significantly more importance than its content or indeed its effect.

src: Eventually Helen got through; Louise was in a meeting, she was told, and could not be reached.

src: Mostly they'd make for the West End and meet others like themselves; they'd pick up survival information, get oriented within the subculture that they'd entered, and learn where the free food could be picked up.

src: She said, do me a favour and get off my back!

src: Martinho encouraged him in this opinion-he liked to make out that the organisation had been moderate until hijacked by extremists who believed in violence.

src: God knew what effect this would have on Mrs Browning, but as she went to bed that night she had no feelings of optimism left and already there yawned ahead a great void.

src: The affair, dubbed Baftagate by members of the British Academy of Film and Television Arts, is taking on the dimensions of a drama itself.

src: There was good enough reason, especially with the leg injury, to rest for a couple of days, but, with Joan in mind, my plan was to collect my mail, buy food, take on water, have a cup of coffee at the hotel, and be off towards Reggane.
mt: "Había una razón suficiente, especialmente debido a la lesión de la pierna, para descansar unos días, pero, pensando en Joan, mi plan era recoger mi correo, comprar comida, tomar agua, tomar un café en el hotel y dirigirme hacia Reggane."

src: I need time and space not just to get over the appalling things which have happened to me in which you do not seem to show much interest but also to think through where the two of us are at.
mt: "Necesito tiempo y espacio no solo para superar las cosas abominables que han sucedido conmigo en las que no parece que muestres mucho interés, sino también para pensar en dónde estamos dos."

src: This was a memorandum that seemed to have taken on a life of its own: now here, now there, now one, now several, now alone, now gone, now lurking.

src: Even Fergie got a mention in a spurious gag about how he and the Royal got together.

src: The simple racing action was inadequate at full-price, but makes for a fair budget game.

"Pero había momentos en los que él pasaba por sus barreras, y, aunque eran raros, esto era uno de ellos."

src: Right that one you've already done up here haven't you?

src: Edward thought, as he did up his shirt buttons.

mt: "Al salir de Whitstable, Glisseuse cambió de rumbo y se dirigió hacia el Canal de Swale, que conecta la Isla de Sheppey con la costa principal de Kent, un popular corte corto para pequeñas embarcaciones cuando la marea está en el lugar."

src: Following our site meeting, I took on board all the points you made.

src: Well the fact that we've got three there, there there that's how many we got on that card and we were quite happy about that.

src: We were able to get on with our routine work without too much drama unfolding around us, and we started to dream of what we would do when the war was over.
mt: "Podemos continuar con nuestro trabajo rutinario sin demasiada drama alrededor de nosotros, y comenzamos a soñar con lo que haríamos cuando terminara la guerra."

src: He expected to take on at least another four by September and more once the village was fully operational.
mt: "Se esperaba asumir al menos cuatro más para septiembre y más una vez que la aldea estuviera completamente operativa."

src: It's ironic that a prostitute can get a hotel room by bribing someone but if you're a respectable woman you are likely to be arrested if you even try to get through the front door.

src: I pretended not to notice her look of astonishment and got on with my shining.

src: I could not make out whether his conscience was troubling him or whether he didn't realise what he was telling me.

src: He will take on a role as the head of a special unit in a hospital, and they will need to adopt the roles of experts whose help is needed in dealing with a particular patient whose case is causing him great concern.
mt: "Él asumirá un papel como jefe de una unidad especial en un hospital, y necesitarán adoptar los roles de expertos cuya ayuda es necesaria para tratar a un paciente en particular cuyo caso les causa gran preocupación."

src: The desire to teach the experts a lesson took on manic proportions.
mt: "El deseo de enseñar a los expertos una lección tomó proporciones maníacas."

src: Anyway, I got through this tricky interval, and even the sea co-operated for once, coming in just after the explosion and sweeping away any tell-tale tracks I might have left an hour or more before Diggs arrived from the village to inspect the scene.
mt: "De todas formas, logré pasar por este intervalo complicado, y por primera vez, el mar cooperó, llegando justo después de la explosión y arrastrando cualquier rastro que pudiera haber dejado una hora o más antes de que Diggs llegara desde el pueblo para inspeccionar el lugar."

src: I think talking to them helped me a lot to get over those first few months.
mt: Creo que hablar con ellos me ayudó mucho a superar los primeros meses.

src: As a result, people in these institutions quickly came to take on the roles and goals which these institutions required for their survival.
mt: "Como resultado, las personas en estas instituciones rápidamente asumieron los roles y objetivos que estas instituciones requerían para su supervivencia."

src: You can pack your bags and get out.

src: Noreen was kept on after the try-out week, but she knew that was only on the strength of Fred, the Stage Manager's sympathetic report, and for the fact that she got on well with everyone in the company, especially Bernie.

src: A defendant whose doctor alleges that the plaintiff is malingering must be prepared to call that doctor at trial to make out the allegation and to face cross-examination.

src: Kiss is about as valid as a football game; it's a release, a chance for people to forget about their problems for a couple of hours, get together in some celebration and loose off some pent-up energy.

src: The owner, a bachelor in his fifties, slept on a camp-bed in the vestibule of the hostel and cooked on a two ring stove in the courtyard, mostly a kind of vegetable stew as far as I could make out.
mt: El propietario, un soltero en sus cincuentas, durmió en una cama de campaña en el vestíbulo del albergue y cocinó en una estufa de dos anillos en el patio, principalmente una especie de guiso de verduras según pude averiguar.

src: And I think it would actually need, if it's going to be taken on board, somebody appointed to look at this, or it be put in somebody's job brief and I would have thought ideally that it would be placed with the community worker, and I don't know quite how we're placed for community workers in East Oxford, but maybe it's something we could put to that department.
mt: "Y creo que realmente necesitaría, si va a ser aceptado, que alguien lo revisara, o que se incluya en el currículum de alguien, y creo que idealmente debería colocarse con el trabajador comunitario, pero no sé exactamente cómo se asignan para los trabajadores comunitarios en East Oxford, pero tal vez podríamos plantearlo a ese departamento."

src: Similarly there seem to be many cultures in tropical countries where the women get on with the necessary work while the men sit around discussing matters.

src: Something at the core of him gave in to her.

src: They're not really severe at all, I mean, they're very much intended for, you know, the weekend er type of people, you know, somebody that just wants to get out on a Sunday afternoon with the children.

src: That said, we never just leave people to get on with it.

src: I only hope that the police committee will actually support those and get on towards introducing them, because that is a way of using some of the people in our village communities to erm, to keep an eye on the community and see that those people that shouldn't go round.
mt: "Solo espero que el comité de policía realmente apoye a esas personas y se acerque a introducirlas, porque es una forma de utilizar a algunas personas de nuestras comunidades rurales para, eh, vigilar la comunidad y asegurarse de que esas personas no vayan por ahí."

mt: "El Gerente Tommy Gilmour volará a Puerto Rico en tres semanas para intentar traer la pelea Camacho a Glasgow en enero, dando casi a Weir casi tres meses para recuperarse de su primer corte en 167 combates como amateur y profesional."

src: Just see how you get on.

src: Dr Barnardo's was another charity she took on at about the same time; this charity has left its old orphanage image behind, and the Princess feels her connections with it have been fruitful.
mt: "Dr Barnardo's fue otra caridad que tomó en serio alrededor del mismo tiempo; esta caridad ha dejado atrás su imagen de antiguo orfanato, y la Princesa siente que sus conexiones con ella han sido fructíferas."

src: She always had on a brown cotton smock which was pinched in around the waist with a wide leather belt.

src: They got onto Timmy just as things were beginning to move.

src: Seven years ago, needing more space for their family (two daughters, currently both art students) and their work, they decided to move, and to take on a rather dilapidated but unrestored farmhouse.

src: Who was I to be taking on a contender like Thomas Carter, a management consultant and the owner-occupier of a £500,000 property set in the accessible Arcadia of Boars Hill?

src: In the cave itself, bas-reliefs sculpted close together on a stalagmite cone, hard to make out for the most part, except for an obvious and memorable reindeer some three feet long, in the museum, animals graphically carved or engraved on bone, many of them heads of horses, but fish too, and pieces of bone, antler and ivory carved quite elaborately into abstract patterns of diamond shapes, chevrons or spirals.
mt: "En el propio caverna, relieves tallados muy cerca unos unos en la base de un cono de estalactita, difíciles de distinguir en su mayoría, excepto por un claro y memorable reno de tres pies de largo, en el museo, animales grabados o tallados gráficamente en hueso, muchos de ellos cráneos de caballos, pero también peces, y piezas de hueso, asta y marfil tallados con gran detalle en patrones abstractos de formas de diamantes, chevrones o espirales."

src: She parked outside the high brick wall rather than driving on to the forecourt, and as if he sensed her reluctance to enter the house again he didn't attempt to invite her inside — but neither did he make any attempt to get out of the car, and they sat in silence in the light from an overhead street-lamp.
mt: Ella estacionó fuera de la alta pared de ladrillo en lugar de conducir hasta el vestíbulo, y como si sintiera su reticencia para entrar en la casa de nuevo, no intentó invitarla adentro — pero tampoco intentó salir del coche, y permanecieron en silencio bajo la luz de una lámpara de calle sobre el tejado.

src: We also have a mobile disenchanted society in which people do get on their bikes, move around from bedsitter to bedsitter with growing elements of homelessness and there are serious problems about registration.
mt: También tenemos una sociedad móvil desilusionada en la que las personas se ponen en bicicleta, se desplazan de un dormitorio a otro con elementos crecientes de sin techo y hay problemas serios sobre la inscripción.

src: In the meantime one studies them as landscapes, so to speak, simply to heighten one's pleasure in sight-seeing, to get behind the superficial appearances, to uncover the layers of the palimpsest and to see, for example, a piece of the tenth century in the way a street makes an abrupt turn or does something else unexpected.
mt: "En el medio, uno los estudia como paisajes, así que se dice, simplemente para aumentar su placer al viajar, para ir más allá de las apariencias superficiales, para descubrir las capas del palimpsesto y, por ejemplo, ver un pedazo del siglo X de la manera en que una calle hace un giro brusco o hace otra cosa inesperada."

mt: Finn debe haber estado despierto en la oscuridad, como ella, la pared que los separaba como la espada de Tristan, porque podía escuchar el suave murmullo de él y Francie hablando unos minutos, pero no podía entender una sola palabra.

src: Er i this is why it's important that you mix with people, that you get out and about, because if you find that everybody else is, is getting up the hill faster than you and everybody else is crossing the roads quicker than you and all the rest of it, it should tell you something, now I mean I don't even want to go into competition but I think it is good occasionally to see if you can walk as fast as that eighteen year old in front of you.
mt: "Él es por eso que es importante que te mezcles con las personas, que salgas y vayas deambulando, porque si descubres que todos los demás están subiendo la colina más rápido que tú y todos los demás están cruzando las carreteras más rápido que tú y todo lo demás, debería decirte algo, ahora bien, no quiero entrar en competencia, pero creo que es bueno ocasionalmente ver si puedes caminar tan rápido como el diecinueveañero delante de ti."

src: So that takes on board suggestions that they are attributed to them.

src: Two thousand more workers are expected to be taken on and the local economy should get a £100 million boost once production reaches capacity.
mt: "Se espera que se tomen dos mil más trabajadores y la economía local debería recibir un impulso de £100 millones una vez que la producción alcance su capacidad."

mt: "Puede ser un clásico clásico, un pequeño caserón aleatorio, una alta tecnología moderna o una visión personal intensamente intensa de Bizancio, que nadie ha imaginado antes, pero que existe en tu cabeza y quiere salir."

mt: Ella no pudo negárselo, por lo tanto, y no lo hizo; sin embargo, más y más parecía que se había rendido a las demandas de Mr. Poole; un hombre era mucho como otro.

src: Other traffic on the road always worried me, so I would stop and get off until the way was clear.

src: Yes, er er I mean I agree with you Chairman entirely, I mean the arguments have been made before and I will just say them again, that I think the District Council are quite remiss er in asking for this sum of money, particularly in view of the reason that the, the, the field is there, which is for the recreation of the people who live in the estate, and the back end of Southwell, and it always has been, er and that is a responsibility that has been taken on by this Parish Council, and if, if, if they feel that they, they wish to persist in asking an unreasonable price, and let's face it, the District Council is in a position to look at this situation and say, the Parish Council is doing an excellent job, we will sell them this land for a figure that they can afford.
mt: Sí, yo, er, yo, me acuerdo, estoy de acuerdo con usted, el Presidente, yo, er, yo, solo voy a repetir lo que se ha dicho antes, creo que el Consejo del Distrito es bastante remiso al pedir esta suma de dinero, especialmente considerando el motivo de que el campo existe, que es para el recreo de las personas que viven en el dominio, y en la parte trasera de Southwell, y siempre ha sido así, er, y eso es una responsabilidad que ha asumido este Consejo Parroquial, y si, si, si sienten que desean seguir pidiendo un precio irrazonable, y pongámoslo así, el Consejo del Distrito está en una posición para mirar esta situación y decir, el Consejo Parroquial está haciendo un excelente trabajo, vendemosles este terreno por un precio que puedan pagar.

mt: "Robertson estaba hablando sobre el orgullo profesional en lugar de sumergirse en un exceso de sentimentalismo cuando catalogó las razones por las cuales solo había expectación entusiasta del amistoso encuentro que ha adquirido una apariencia hostil desde que la lesión devastó la primera selección de jugadores de Roxburgh."

src: Most of the leading academics in business ethics have a grounding in moral philosophy, a discipline that takes the concept of altruism — doing good simply because it is right — rather seriously.
mt: "La mayoría de los académicos líderes en ética empresarial tienen una base en la filosofía moral, una disciplina que toma en serio el concepto de altruismo — hacer el bien simplemente porque es correcto."

src: Round Table Day is made for Table and Table only and has all the best features which are inherent in this movement.
mt: "El Día de la Mesa Redonda está hecho solo para Mesa y Mesa, y tiene todas las mejores características que son inherentes a este movimiento."

src: Just get on with it.
mt: "Solo adelante."

src: Our problem is we can't get at the buyers.
mt: "Nuestra dificultad es que no podemos llegar a los compradores."

src: She had on all her jewellery and a great fur cloak over her nightdress.
mt: Ella llevaba puesta toda su joyería y una gran capa de piel de oso encima de su bata de noche.

src: Anyone who takes on public duties sets himself up for attack, and in Rees-Mogg's case, the succession of posts was dizzying.
mt: "Cualquiera que asuma deberes públicos se pone a sí mismo en riesgo, y en el caso de Rees-Mogg, la sucesión de puestos fue vertiginosa."

src: But you see, what we've got is a set of fairly standard steps here which you got through no matter what the project and I hope that er, you've all had a bit of practice at that.
mt: Pero ves, lo que tenemos aquí es un conjunto de pasos bastante estándar que has pasado por alto, sin importar el proyecto, y espero que, en general, hayas tenido un poco de práctica en eso.

src: You can't get out.
mt: "No puedes salir."

src: Yeah I never thought of that, yeah I suppose its colder you don't have to take that many clothes out there do you, cos get out there
mt: Sí, nunca pensé en eso, sí, supongo que es más frío allá, ¿no tienes que sacar tantas cosas? Porque salgas ahí.

src: I would never take on an act who couldn't perform live.
mt: "Jamás tomaría un acto que no pudiera actuar en vivo."

src: When we had finally retrieved all his excess baggage, it took us even longer than in Mexico to clear customs because he insisted on unrolling a big holdall right there on the bench to get at his oilskins.
mt: "Cuando finalmente recuperamos todos sus equipajes de exceso, nos tomó incluso más tiempo que en México para pasar por aduanas porque insistió en desplegar un gran baúl allí en la banca para acceder a sus impermeables."

src: he gets on with it
mt: "se lleva bien con ello"

src: JE: Joan, were you at all reluctant to take on this major role so late in your career?
mt: JE: Joan, ¿estabas en absoluto reacio a asumir este papel principal más tarde en tu carrera?

src: He didn't get out much.
mt: "No salió mucho."

src: If she wants whatever it is she gets out of this relationship, no one can deny she's entitled to it.
mt: "Si ella quiere lo que obtiene de esta relación, nadie puede negar que tiene derecho a ello."

src: A major inquiry was launched immediately into how the bombers got through to plant the device in a hut used by police on security duty outside the Bishop Street courthouse.
mt: "Se inició de inmediato una importante investigación para averiguar cómo los bombarderos lograron llegar a plantar el dispositivo en una cabaña utilizada por la policía en servicio de seguridad fuera del juzgado de la calle Bishop."

src: If there was trouble he'd have to spend the rest of the day putting it down and wouldn't be able to get on with the arms search at all.
mt: "Si hubiera problemas, tendría que pasar el resto del día arreglándolos y no podría continuar con la búsqueda de las armas en absoluto."

src: talking, all messing each other bits up, you had about six spread round your room for that time, and it was so good and we got through it so quick, there were so few mistakes because you only ever had six maximum kids in your room at once at that was only because Terry offered to put on a video an and
mt: Hablando, todos se estaban metiendo en los bolsillos unos a otros, había alrededor de seis niños repartidos por tu habitación en ese momento, y era genial, nos lo pasamos rápido con muy pocas equivocaciones, porque solo podías tener un máximo de seis niños en tu habitación a la vez, y solo porque Terry se ofreció a poner un video.

src: I 'ad a long stint wiv Galloway an' I was gutted when I got the push an' we 'ad ter get out o' the 'ouse.
mt: "Le 'ad un largo período con Galloway y me entristeció cuando recibí la orden y nos 'ad que fuéramos a salir de la casa."

src: Jeff Hammond, of Willis Corroon, said the service would provide the means of dealing with these potential threats to businesses enabling them to get on with their main task — providing quality products to an ever more aware consumer market.
mt: "Jeff Hammond, de Willis Corroon, dijo que el servicio proporcionaría los medios para enfrentar estos posibles amenazas a las empresas, permitiéndoles continuar con su principal tarea — proporcionar productos de calidad a un mercado consumidor cada vez más consciente."

src: You have only to give in a few times (out of many when you stick to your planned ignoring) to lose the battle.
mt: "Solo tienes que rendirte unas pocas veces (de muchas cuando te adhieres a tu plan de ignorancia) para perder la batalla."

src: We can't get off it.
mt: No podemos salir de eso.

src: The planning of operations by those who are going to carry them out, obviates delay and misunderstandings which happen to be caused by intermediate stages, and makes for the speed of execution which in any operation of this kind is an incalculable asset.
mt: "La planificación de las operaciones por aquellos que van a llevarlas a cabo, obvia los retrasos y malentendidos causados por etapas intermedias, y hace posible la velocidad de ejecución, que en cualquier operación de este tipo es un activo incalculable."

src: We have to get off after three weeks.
mt: Tenemos que salir después de tres semanas.

src: One mustn't give in to pessimism, and it's pointless to look too far ahead, but in a couple of years she won't even be legal.
mt: "Se debe no rendirse ante la pesimismo, y es inútil mirar demasiado lejos, pero en unos pocos años ella ya no será legal."

src: What affect has it had on your family and your life.
mt: "¿Qué efecto ha tenido en tu familia y en tu vida?"

src: If you could get on to Basil, then we can get the ball rolling.
mt: "Si pudieras subirte a Basil, entonces podemos empezar a poner en marcha."

src: Pandora, the first woman of Greek myth — as described by Hesiod — is made out of earth; at Zeus's command she was fashioned out of clay to be the instrument of divine retribution.
mt: Pandora, la primera mujer de la mitología griega — como se describe por Hesíodo — está hecha de tierra; a la orden de Zeus, fue hecha de arcilla para ser el instrumento de la venganza divina.

src: This is also partly the reason why in Adorno's theory music in a sense takes on too much autonomy, so much as to create a danger of a relapse into idealism.
mt: "Esto también es en parte la razón por la cual, en la teoría de Adorno, la música en cierto sentido adquiere demasiada autonomía, hasta el punto de crear un peligro de un resurgimiento del idealismo."

src: I thought so I mean it was getting on a bit
mt: Pensé así, es decir, se estaba poniendo un poco.

src: It is important to be able to recover composure after stress, to forget the low turn, and to get on with making a good landing.
mt: "Es importante poder recuperar la compostura después del estrés, olvidar la mala maniobra y continuar con un buen aterrizaje."

src: I haven't got time to plan it, I'd better get on with it.
mt: "No tengo tiempo para planearlo, mejor seguir adelante con ello."
